meta.name: Soul Food Oxtail Stew
meta.author: SSA Canonical Catalog

source.notes: Canonical SSA soul food baseline. Deeply browned oxtails braised low and slow with aromatics into a rich gravy-style stew. Includes optional thickening step.

meta.summary: Fall-off-the-bone oxtails braised with onion, garlic, herbs, and broth until the sauce turns into a rich, glossy gravy.

notes: Braising is complete when oxtails are very tender and meat releases easily from bone (often 190°F+).
Cool leftovers quickly and refrigerate within 2 hours.

ingredients.label: All-purpose flour (for dredging; optional but helps browning)

steps.title: Season oxtails
steps.body: Pat oxtails dry. Season with salt, pepper, garlic powder, onion powder, and paprika. Optional: lightly dredge in flour, shaking off excess.

steps.title: Brown oxtails deeply
steps.body: Heat oil in a Dutch oven over medium-high. Brown oxtails in batches 3–5 minutes per side until deeply browned. Transfer browned oxtails to a plate.

steps.title: Sauté aromatics
steps.body: Reduce heat to medium. Add onion, celery, and carrot to the pot. Cook 6–8 minutes, scraping up browned bits, until softened. Add garlic and cook 30–60 seconds.

steps.title: Cook tomato paste
steps.body: Stir in tomato paste and cook 1–2 minutes until it darkens slightly and smells roasted.

steps.title: Add broth and braise
steps.body: Return oxtails to the pot. Add broth, Worcestershire, bay leaves, and thyme. Bring to a simmer, then cover and cook on low heat for 2.5–3.5 hours, until very tender. Stir occasionally and keep liquid at a gentle simmer.

steps.title: Reduce sauce
steps.body: Remove lid for the last 20–30 minutes to reduce and thicken the sauce into a gravy-like consistency. Skim excess fat if desired.

steps.title: Optional: thicken with slurry
steps.body: If you want a thicker gravy, whisk cornstarch with water to make a slurry. Stir into simmering sauce and cook 2–4 minutes until thickened.

steps.title: Finish and serve
steps.body: Remove bay leaves. Taste and adjust seasoning. Serve oxtails and gravy over rice, mashed potatoes, or alongside greens and cornbread.
